domain: Chinese culinary craft — jiaozi dumpling pleating (10-13 small directional pleats along one edge of a circular wrapper, sealing the filling; back side flat for pan contact; pleats trap steam pockets)
specific_mechanism: Wrapper is folded in half over filling; the front layer is gathered into 10-13 pleats while back stays flat. Pleats all point in one direction (creating a curved crescent). Pleats serve two functions: (1) mechanically seal the filling (each pleat is a folded-over local seal); (2) trap steam pockets that improve cooking. The asymmetric design (flat back, pleated front) is intentional: flat back gives crispy contact in pan-fry; pleated front gives steam-pocket retention.
llm_application: Asymmetric prompt-wrap with directional pleats: when wrapping a complex query in a structured prompt, create a flat 'back surface' (system prompt + task description) and a pleated 'front surface' (the query + N=5-13 directional structured-output instructions, each pleat folding the spec back over the query). The directional pleats are NOT redundant — each pleat instructs the model on a different aspect (format, tone, evidence-required, citation-style, length, conditioning, etc.) all pointing in the same direction (toward a single coherent output). The flat back gives reliable parsing; the pleated front gives nuanced compliance.
novelty_claim: Structured-prompt research (DSPy, content-format integrated 2502.04295) and hierarchical prompts (2305.14257) compose multiple prompt elements. None implements an ASYMMETRIC pleat-pattern: flat-back-system + directional-pleated-front with each pleat being a single-instruction directional fold. The 'flat-back / pleated-front' duality is the candidate's distinct geometric framing.
